budget: One bounded Codex implementation card for Linear META-18. Establish an explicit, machine-readable execution-boundary contract for every coding CLI adapter without pretending the post-hoc verifier sandbox protects generation: Codex CODE_EDIT uses its enforced native workspace-write mode; subscription Codex remains native read-only; subscription Claude remains plan mode with Read/Glob/Grep; Pi, OpenCode, and edit-capable Claude are explicitly classified as operator-trusted CLI boundaries with no harness filesystem jail. Emit the selected boundary before spawn and document the per-adapter guarantees and limitations. Add a cross-process exclusive lease keyed by the resolved workspace path so concurrent workers cannot mutate or inspect one shared checkout while an attempt is active; separate explicitly supplied git worktrees remain supported, but the harness does not auto-create branches or worktrees. Activate scoped capability tokens at the TaskExecutor pre-dispatch boundary: mint and validate a short-lived token bound to worker, task, task:execute, tier, and task type before attempt assignment/start events and before runner.run; invalid, expired, revoked, wrong-subject, wrong-task, or wrong-scope authorization must fail closed with no worker call, no capability evidence, and reasoned provenance/event evidence. Preserve the existing post-hoc OS-sandboxed verifier, result authenticity checks, routing, budgets, context assembly, human approvals, and adapter behavior outside these explicit boundaries.
stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on any evaluator, merge, promotion, deployment, credential, or runtime-authority grant; H/E/W drift; owned-path expansion; an authorization failure that still invokes a runner or emits attempt.started; a token not bound to the chosen worker and task; a shared-workspace collision that permits concurrent execution; a claim implementation that auto-creates, deletes, resets, or switches git worktrees; documentation that calls operator-trusted Pi/OpenCode/Claude execution sandboxed; credential removal or network denial that breaks authenticated CLI use; weakening the protected verifier sandbox or result-signature checks; failing acceptance commands; or unresolved independent-review P0/P1 findings.
evaluatorAuthority: The implementing seat cannot self-approve. Authorization validity is deterministic signature, expiry, revocation, subject, task, and scope validation. Workspace exclusivity is tested with real overlapping attempts and a cross-process lock probe. Adapter-boundary claims are checked against exact argv and emitted evidence; operator-trusted adapters remain honestly labeled. Coordinator integration requires frozen focused/full test evidence plus a read-only Pi/NeuralWatt GLM-5.2 frozen-diff review with no unresolved P0/P1 findings. Human promotion and deployment authority remain unchanged.
nextCheckpoint: Invalid-input tests prove an invalid scoped dispatch token produces zero runner calls and no attempt.started event; overlapping workers targeting one resolved workspace yield one winner and one reasoned workspace_claim denial while distinct worktrees remain independent; every adapter reports an honest execution-boundary classification matching its argv; focused tests pass.
